title: alchemilla glaberrima subsp. glabra
intro: Alchemilla glaberrima subsp. glabra is a subspecies of the alpine meadow milkwort plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It is native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems, due to its ability to stimulate collagen production and soothe inflammation. Alchemilla glaberrima subsp. glabra can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and salves to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
